jokes.text: We saw this coming a mile away, and we've had our hearts set on this boat thing for days now, which, in our world, is a level of focus in which I personally have never experienced.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis

dialogue.line: Fire's getting bigger. Fire's getting big.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Bail!

jokes.text: Find some water. Find some water?! Find some water. What do you mean find some water? There's water everywhere. Help! Help us.
jokes.setup: Frank telling Charlie to find water for the fire
jokes.punchline: Charlie pointing out they're literally surrounded by water
jokes.explanation: Panic making them stupid - surrounded by water but can't figure out how to use it for firefighting

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Find some water. Find some water?! Find some water.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: What do you mean find some water? There's water everywhere.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Help! Help us.

jokes.text: It's so much more romantic in the middle of nowhere where we can be completely alone, and you can make rash decisions based on fear. Right, Dennis?
jokes.setup: Mac talking to women at party
jokes.punchline: Accidentally using Dennis's predatory language verbatim
jokes.explanation: Mac sabotaging himself by repeating Dennis's terrible pickup strategy word for word

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: It's so much more romantic in the middle of nowhere where we can be completely alone, and you can make rash decisions based on fear. Right, Dennis?

jokes.text: I thought we told you guys to beat it. Out of my way. No more Diddy boat. Damn catfish nipping at my heels all the w... Endangered my ass. There's a billion of them out there.
jokes.setup: Frank and Charlie arriving at party soaking wet
jokes.punchline: Frank complaining about the 'endangered' catfish attacking him during their escape swim
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback - Frank's earlier dismissal of environmental warnings proven wrong when fish actually attack him

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: I thought we told you guys to beat it. Out of my way.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: No more Diddy boat. Damn catfish nipping at my heels all the w... Endangered my ass. There's a billion of them out there.

jokes.text: Damn catfish nipping at my heels all the way. Endangered my ass. There's a billion of them out there.
jokes.setup: Frank emerges from his swim
jokes.punchline: His continued denial about the catfish being endangered despite being chased by them
jokes.explanation: Frank's stubbornness in the face of contrary evidence is consistent character work

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Damn catfish nipping at my heels all the way. Endangered my ass. There's a billion of them out there.

jokes.text: Charlie, where's the boat? What happened to the boat? It's right there. This was a terrible investment.
jokes.setup: Looking for their boat after the fire
jokes.punchline: Charlie pointing to burning/destroyed wreckage, Frank's massive understatement
jokes.explanation: Perfect ending - their entire venture literally goes up in smoke, Frank's understated business assessment

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Charlie, where's the boat? What happened to the boat?

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: It's right there.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: This was a terrible investment.
